timestamp_shape: BSD (Mmm dd HH:MM:SS, no year, no TZ)
notes: Default ASA 'logging host' output when PRI is stripped by the receiver or when ASA is configured without PRI. Tag is '%ASA-{sev}-{msgid}:'; body is free-form key/value and positional text, not CEF/LEEF. Hostname field may be a hostname or dotted-IP. No year and no timezone in BSD timestamp.
canonical_examples: Jan 15 10:15:30 FIREWALL-1 %ASA-1-101001: (Primary) Failover cable OK.
Jan 24 03:23:30 192.168.1.1 %ASA-6-302013: Built outbound TCP connection 506986
Dec 31 06:15:30 FIREWALL-1 %ASA-2-106001: Inbound TCP connection denied from 10.0.0.1/25000 to 192.168.1.1/443 flags URG SYN RST on interface OUTSIDE

timestamp_shape: BSD (Mmm dd HH:MM:SS, no year, no TZ)
notes: Wire form when ASA forwards to a remote syslog collector over UDP/TCP 514. PRI encodes facility (often local4/local7, configurable via 'logging facility') and severity mirroring the %ASA-N- digit. Tag 'mnemonic' follows colon-space.
canonical_examples: <166>Jan 9 23:16:13 ca-edge-fw %ASA-6-106100: access-list inbound permitted tcp outside/10.0.0.5(54321) -> inside/192.168.1.10(443) hit-cnt 1
<167>Jan 9 23:16:08 ca-edge-fw %ASA-7-713035: Group = 10.0.0.1, IP = 10.0.0.1, Received remote Proxy Host data in ID Payload

timestamp_shape: RFC5424 (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) when 'logging timestamp rfc5424' is set; BSD otherwise
notes: EMBLEM format ('logging emblem'): a colon immediately follows the PRI ('<166>:'), then the timestamp, another ': ', then the %ASA tag. Hostname is omitted from the header; device identity comes from an optional 'device-id' appended earlier in the pipeline or from the source IP. Use of RFC5424 timestamp is a separate toggle from EMBLEM.
canonical_examples: <166>:2018-06-27T12:17:46Z: %ASA-6-110002: Failed to locate egress interface for protocol from src interface :src IP/src port to dest IP/dest port

timestamp_shape: BSD (Mmm dd HH:MM:SS, no year, no TZ)
notes: PAN-OS default is BSD RFC3164 envelope; body is a CSV record whose first field is the PAN-OS log format version (e.g., '1'), followed by the device-local timestamp and log-type-specific fields. IETF/RFC5424 envelope is also supported as a non-default config; cataloged separately when an example is obtained. Body format (CSV) is out of envelope scope.
canonical_examples: <12>Apr 14 16:48:54 paloalto.test.net 1,2020/04/14 16:48:54,unknown,SYSTEM,auth,0,2020/04/14 16:48:54,,auth-fail,,0,0,general,medium,failed authentication for user 'admin'. Reason: Invalid username/password. From: 10.0.10.55.,1718,0x0,0,0,0,0,,paloalto
<14>Apr 14 16:54:18 paloalto.test.net 1,2020/04/14 16:54:18,unknown,CONFIG,0,0,2020/04/14 16:54:18,10.0.10.55,,set,admin,Web,Succeeded, deviceconfig system,127,0x0,0,0,0,0,,paloalto

notes: RFC3164-style envelope with classic Cisco header layout: timestamp, hostname, then a numeric sequence number preceding the mnemonic. Seqnum present when 'service sequence-numbers' is set. Body tag is '%FACILITY-SEVERITY-MNEMONIC:' followed by free-form text. Common when 'service timestamps log datetime msec' is NOT set; older default form.
canonical_examples: <189>Sep 5 10:22:33 myrouter 4145: %SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list 101 denied tcp 192.168.1.50(55214) -> 10.1.1.1(80), 1 packet

notes: Wire form when 'service timestamps log datetime msec' is set (often with 'service sequence-numbers'). Cisco-specific ordering: optional message-counter ('237: '), optional zero-padded sequence-number ('000485: '), hostname, then a single-char NTP marker ('*' = clock not yet synced, '.' = synced, ' ' = neither displayed in some configs), then BSD timestamp with millisecond fractional seconds. Numeric prefixes are independently togglable; either or both may be absent. Body tag identical to the rfc3164 variant.
canonical_examples: <189>237: 000485: router1: *Jan 8 19:46:03.295: %LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN: Line protocol on Interface Loopback100, changed state to up

notes: IOS-XE-specific format enabled by 'logging trap informational syslog-format rfc5424'. Fully RFC5424-conformant envelope (PRI, version=1, ISO8601 timestamp with optional millisecond fraction, hostname, appname, procid, msgid, structured-data placeholder '-' when unused) followed by Cisco's '%FACILITY-SEVERITY-MNEMONIC:' tag and free-form body. IOS-XE's default standard format inherits the cisco_ios_msec_seqnum_dotmarker variant from IOS; this entry catalogs only the IOS-XE-only RFC5424 mode.
canonical_examples: <45>1 2003-10-11T22:14:15.003Z 10.64.48.125 polaris-user1 - - - %LINK-5-CHANGED: Interface GigabitEthernet0/0, changed state to administratively down

notes: IOS-XR-specific shape with distinctive features: (1) leading numeric sequence number always present; (2) hostname followed by space-separated node-id token like 'RP/0/RSP1/CPU0' or 'LC/0/2/CPU0' (routing-processor / linecard / CPU identifier from XR's distributed OS); (3) node-id is colon-glued to the timestamp without a space ('RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:Mar 28 ...'); (4) optional TZ token (e.g., 'UTC') before the inner colon; (5) process-name with PID in brackets; (6) spaces around the colon separating mnemonic from message body. RFC3164-derived but quirky enough that strict parsers reject it.
canonical_examples: <149>2647599: vmx01 RP/0/RSP1/CPU0:Mar 28 15:08:30.941 UTC: bgp[1051]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-MAXPFX : No. of IPv4 Unicast prefixes received from 1.2.3.4 has reached 94106, max 125000
<187>94307: gw2.acy1 LC/0/2/CPU0:Jul 7 20:16:14.834 : ifmgr[214]: %PKT_INFRA-LINK-3-UPDOWN : Interface TenGigE0/2/0/4, changed state to Down

notes: NX-OS native format. Distinctive ordering and timestamp shape: (1) hostname IMMEDIATELY follows PRI (no timestamp first), colon-terminated; (2) timestamp starts with year then 'Mon dd HH:MM:SS' (year-first, non-BSD non-ISO); (3) TZ token always emitted (e.g., 'UTC'); (4) timestamp also colon-terminated; (5) %FACILITY-SEV-MNEMONIC tag follows. 'logging timestamp [microseconds|milliseconds|seconds]' adjusts precision. Some Nexus releases include slot/module qualifier in the mnemonic like '%FACILITY-SLOTn-SEV-MNEM'. Strict RFC3164 parsers reject this due to year-first timestamp and hostname-first ordering.
canonical_examples: <190>sw01.pdx01: 2017 Jul 28 14:42:46 UTC: %AUTHPRIV-6-SYSTEM_MSG: pam_unix(dcos_sshd:session): session opened for user luke by (uid=0) - dcos_sshd[12977]

notes: Cisco Meraki uses an RFC5424-shaped envelope distinct in two ways: (1) the timestamp position holds a unix-epoch with fractional seconds rather than ISO8601, and (2) following the hostname there's a Meraki-specific event-type token (events, urls, firewall, ip_flow_start, ip_flow_end, vpn_firewall, flows, security_event, etc.) rather than RFC5424's appname/procid/msgid triple. The canonical_examples here are verbatim from Meraki docs which present message bodies WITHOUT the PRI; the actual wire form over UDP prepends standard '<PRI>1 ' framing — e.g., '<134>1 1380664922.583851938 MX84 events type=vpn_connectivity_change'. Default facility tends to be local0/local6 and severity follows event type. Body sub-format after event_type (key=value, free text, or CSV) is out of envelope scope.
canonical_examples: 1380664922.583851938 MX84 events type=vpn_connectivity_change

notes: Cisco IOS/IOS-XE format when 'service timestamps log datetime msec localtime show-timezone' is configured, common on Catalyst 9800 wireless controllers. Distinct from cisco_ios_msec_seqnum_dotmarker by: (1) explicit TZ token (e.g., 'IST', 'UTC', 'PST') after the timestamp, (2) absence of msg_counter and seqnum, (3) absence of hostname and NTP-marker. The body after the mnemonic frequently begins with a Catalyst-9800-specific 'Chassis X R0/0:' identifier and process name (e.g., 'wncd:' for the wireless network control daemon) — body content, not envelope. Canonical example is verbatim from Cisco docs which present console form WITHOUT PRI; wire form over UDP prepends standard '<PRI>' framing.
canonical_examples: Oct 1 14:41:27.785 IST: %CLIENT_ORCH_LOG-7-CLIENT_IP_UPDATED: Chassis 1 R0/0: wncd: Username (dev2), MAC: 0062.xxxx.0077, IP fe80::262:aff:xxxx:77 101.6.2.119

notes: Cisco ISE distinctive multi-segment format: '<PRI>BSD-ts ip_or_host CISE_Category msg_id total_seg seg_num <body>'. CISE category names like CISE_Passed_Authentications, CISE_Failed_Attempts, CISE_RADIUS_Accounting, CISE_PassiveID indicate event class. Long messages may be split across multiple syslog records — total_seg gives total number of segments and seg_num gives this one's index (0-based). Body has its own ISO8601 timestamp + sequence number + severity (NOTICE/INFO/WARN/ERROR) + categorized text + key=value attributes. Note that the inner-format timestamp may differ from the wire timestamp because the body was generated locally and the wire timestamp may be added at forwarding time. Doc presents pri-as-number-not-bracketed; canonical_example here prepends '<134>' as the implied wire form of the doc-listed example.
canonical_examples: <134>Dec 9 17:30:23 example CISE_Passed_Authentications 0000453439 1 0 2025-12-09 17:30:23.365 +01:00 0007121364 5200 NOTICE Passed-Authentication: Authentication succeeded, ConfigVersionId=10

notes: Cisco Cyber Vision OT/ICS visibility platform. Wire form: '<PRI>ISO8601-with-fractional-and-tz <process>[pid]: CEF:0|Cisco|Cyber Vision|...'. Distinctive: NO hostname between timestamp and process tag — 'Center' is the process name (not hostname), 'cybervision[pid]' is sub-process. Body is CEF — out of envelope scope. ISO8601 timestamp includes microsecond fractional and timezone offset. Documentation shows a wrapped form where a syslog-ng-style relay prepends 'Aug 1 07:51:21 10.106.15.42 ' to the original Cyber Vision message — that prefix is added by the relay, not Cyber Vision itself. Canonical example here is the inner Cyber Vision wire form (without the relay prefix); doc form shows no PRI so '<134>' is the implied wire framing.
canonical_examples: <134>2025-08-01T11:46:03.329144+00:00 Center cybervision[1]: CEF:0|Cisco|Cyber Vision|2.0|alert_raised|Severe vulnerabilities in monitored entities|1|cat=Vulnerability msg=A severe vulnerability has been detected on a monitored asset alertId=10db6ad6-4968-4b56-98d8-e2d003ea1959

notes: Classic RFC3164 'Unix daemon' wire shape: '<PRI>BSD-ts hostname process[pid]: body'. This is the most common syslog form across Unix/Linux daemons (sshd, postfix, etc.) — Stealthwatch (formerly Lancope) is the citable Cisco example. Distinct from asa_bsd_with_pri (tag = '%ASA-N-MSG:' style) and cisco_ios_rfc3164_seqnum_after_host (extra seqnum field) by the 'processname[pid]:' tag convention. Body in this example is CEF — body format (CEF/LEEF/KV/etc.) is out of envelope scope. Many Cisco appliances running Linux internals, ISE-adjacent forwarders, and most Unix daemons emit this shape. Future firings finding this exact shape on other products (Postfix, sshd, etc.) should defer to discovered.jsonl referencing this entry.
canonical_examples: <131>Jan 12 23:31:01 hostname1 Stealthwatch[233]: CEF:0|Cisco|Stealthwatch|7.3.2|Notification:40|Suspect Data Loss|4|msg=Indicates that an inside host has uploaded an abnormal amount of data to Outside hosts.

notes: Junos standard syslog format (RFC3164) — distinguished from generic RFC3164 by the two-colon-separated tag convention: '<PRI>BSD-ts hostname TAG_NAME: SUB_TAG: body'. The first tag is the system-message-tag (e.g., RT_FLOW for security flow events, MIB_SNMP, KERN_SLP_NEW_TIMER); the second is the specific event identifier (e.g., RT_FLOW_SESSION_CREATE, RT_FLOW_SESSION_CLOSE). Consistent across Junos OS running on MX/PTX routers, SRX firewalls (security/traffic logs in standard mode via 'set security log mode event'), EX/QFX switches. Body free-form text or space-separated key=value depending on event class. Junos also supports RFC5424 'sd-syslog' structured format ('<14>1 2012-11-18T09:56:58.806-07:00 INTERNET-ROUTER RT_FLOW - RT_FLOW_SESSION_CREATE [junos@2636.1.1.1.2.41 source-address=...]') — that envelope is RFC5424 (matches ios_xe_rfc5424 shape with structured-data populated rather than '-'). The standard form here is the more common default.
canonical_examples: <14>Nov 4 16:23:09 cixi RT_FLOW: RT_FLOW_SESSION_CREATE: session created

notes: Juniper NetScreen ScreenOS legacy format — distinctive in that it omits a timestamp from the envelope entirely (time appears as start_time= inside the body). Wire form: '<PRI>hostname: NetScreen device_id=DEVID [DOMAIN]CATEGORY-NUMERIC_ID(class): body-with-kv-pairs'. The 'NetScreen' literal string is always present. The bracketed [DOMAIN] (e.g., [Root]) is the security domain. Category like 'system-notification' or 'traffic' followed by '-NNNNN' message ID then '(class)'. Body uses space-separated key=value with quoted strings. timestamp_shape='other' because the envelope itself has no timestamp — wire-time can be derived only from syslog-server receipt or from the start_time= body field. ScreenOS reached EOL in 2017; legacy systems may still emit this format.
canonical_examples: <133>netscr_pri_01: NetScreen device_id=nsisg2000a [Root]system-notification-00257(traffic): start_time="2014-04-11 14:11:08" duration=0 policy_id=1245 service=syslog proto=17 src zone=Global dst zone=Global action=Deny sent=0 rcvd=390 src=1.2.3.4 dst=5.6.7.8 src_port=50000 dst_port=500

notes: Aruba ClearPass Policy Manager (NAC) format. Distinctive timestamp shape: 'Mmm dd YYYY HH:MM:SS[.SSS] [TZ]' — BSD-month-day combined with year, optional millisecond fractional, and optional timezone token. Not directly representable in the timestamp_shape enum (combination of bsd_with_year + bsd_fractional + bsd_tz), so 'other' with full description here. After timestamp+tz: source-IP-or-hostname, then CEF body. CEF body is out of envelope scope but distinctive vendor=='Aruba Networks' product=='ClearPass'. Multiple syslog export filters supported (Audit, Session, Insight); all share this envelope. PRI usually <143>=local1.warning or <134>=local0.info but configurable.
canonical_examples: <143>May 09 2022 02:32:55 10.10.1.2 CEF:0|Aruba Networks|ClearPass|6.7.8.109113|2001|Failed Authentications|5|
Dec 03 2017 16:31:28.861 IST 10.17.4.208 CEF:0|Aruba Networks|ClearPass|6.5.0.69058|0-1-0|Insight Logs|0|

notes: Arista EOS Linux-based switch OS format. Distinctive: '<PRI>BSD-ts hostname process_name: %FACILITY-SEV-MNEMONIC: body'. The process name (e.g., 'Ebra' for Ethernet agent, 'Stp' for spanning tree, 'Aaa' for AAA) does NOT have '[pid]' brackets, distinguishing it from rfc3164_unix_proc_pid. Then a Cisco-style %FACILITY-SEV-MNEMONIC: tag follows. Many Arista mnemonics intentionally mirror Cisco's (LINEPROTO, LLDP, SYS) for migration ease. Doc shows console form WITHOUT PRI; '<189>' is reconstructed wire form (local7.notice typical).
canonical_examples: <189>Mar 27 21:55:22 se515 Ebra: %LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN: Line protocol on Interface Ethernet22, changed state

notes: Arista CloudVision Portal RFC5424-ish format. Distinctive: (1) SPACE after PRI before timestamp (NO version digit '1' as in standard RFC5424); (2) timestamp uses 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.<nanoseconds> +<tz_offset> <tz_name>' high-precision form (NOT canonical RFC5424 ISO8601 with 'T' separator and 'Z'); (3) hostname, category (e.g., 'changecontrol', 'configlet', 'audit'), then 'Message:' literal, then variable-content body with field labels like 'User:', 'DeviceName:'. CVP also supports a BSD format ('<166>Dec 20 16:09:41 cvp270.hostname.com configlet Message: ...') — that BSD-mode form matches the Arista EOS arista_eos_proc_cisco_tag envelope shape (process without [pid], without %TAG). The high-precision 5424-ish form here is the more distinctive of the two CVP modes.
canonical_examples: <166> 2021-12-20 16:10:00.280604274 +0000 UTC cvp270.hostname.com changecontrol Message: Copying running config to start up config for device esx25-v2-vm7.hostname.com, User: admin, DeviceName: esx25-v2-vm7.hostname.com

notes: HPE Comware (H3C) format. Distinctive: '<PRI>%%vv <BSD-ts:fractional> YYYY <sysname> <MODULE>/<level>/<DIGEST>: <content>'. The literal '%%10' (where vv=10 is current version of Comware syslog) appears only when output destination is log host (not in local log buffer). Timestamp uses colon-separated fractional seconds 'HH:MM:SS:fff' (NOT '.fff' as in BSD/ISO) followed by space then year. Module/level/digest is a Comware-specific tag where module is the subsystem (e.g., DEVD, IFNET, OSPF), level is severity 0-7, digest is up to 32-char message identifier. timestamp_shape='other' due to the unusual ':fff' fractional separator and trailing year position. Local log buffer form (without %%10) starts with '%' character followed by the timestamp.
canonical_examples: <189>%%10 Apr 2 11:38:11:113 2010 H3C DEVD/3/BOARD REBOOT:Chasis 0 slot 2 need be rebooted automatically!

notes: Extreme Networks WiNG (Motorola wireless heritage; controllers, service platforms, APs) format. Distinctive ordering: '<PRI>BSD-ts YYYY: %FACILITY-SEV-MNEMONIC: process[pid]: body'. Combines Cisco-style %TAG with Unix-style process[pid]: in the same envelope, with the %TAG appearing BEFORE the process tag (reverse of typical Unix daemon layout). Year is appended to the BSD timestamp inside the same colon-terminated field. No hostname in this canonical example — may be omitted or replaced by the process tag. Common WiNG mnemonics: %USER, %CFGD (config daemon), %DOT11 (802.11 wireless events), %SECMSG (security), %SYS, %DEV. Doc shows console form WITHOUT PRI; '<27>' is reconstructed wire form (user.err typical given '%USER-3-ERR').
canonical_examples: <27>Aug 23 13:01:21 2019: %USER-3-ERR: Unspec[1824]: Unable to send message to subscribe for event rim_up.

notes: Dell SmartFabric OS10 (modern Linux-based switching OS replacing FTOS) format. Distinctive: '<PRI>BSD-ts %Node.X-Unit.Y:severity:hostname %log-<level>:EVENT_NAME body'. The '%Node.N-Unit.N' identifies the stack member / chassis unit (with '%' prefix); a numeric severity follows after a colon (Dell docs render this as the literal 'PRI' placeholder in some examples but the wire form has the real severity digit 0-7 there); hostname is a separate token before the second tag '%log-<severity>' where <severity> is a word like 'notice', 'warning', 'err'. Then a colon and the event name (ETL_SERVICE_UP, LINK_DOWN, etc.). Dell community example shows the doc-placeholder form 'Jun 1 05:01:46 %Node.1-Unit.1:PRI:OS10 %log-notice:ETL_SERVICE_UP' — the 'PRI' there is documentation-only, not literal wire bytes. Canonical_example here normalizes that placeholder to '5' (notice level).
canonical_examples: <189>Jun 1 05:01:46 %Node.1-Unit.1:5:OS10 %log-notice:ETL_SERVICE_UP

notes: Dell PowerSwitch N-series (legacy small/midsize switching, replacing PowerConnect) format. Multiple distinctive features: (1) space immediately after PRI before timestamp ('<189> JUN' not '<189>JUN'), (2) uppercase month abbreviation ('JUN' not 'Jun'), (3) hostname/IP with stack-unit suffix '10.10.10.13-2' indicating switch unit 2 of stack 10.10.10.13, (4) process name with very large numeric PID 'TRAPMGR[123456789]:', (5) source-file reference 'traputil.c(611)' with line number, (6) numeric message-id, (7) literal '%%' double-percent separator before the human-readable body. Per Splunk's SC4S parser docs, the leading-space-after-PRI quirk may break strict RFC3164 parsers expecting no whitespace between PRI and timestamp. Default port 514, BSD legacy framing.

MikroTik RouterOS minimal-RFC3164 shape: '<PRI>BSD-ts hostname body' — NO process tag, NO Cisco-style %FACILITY-SEV-MNEMONIC, just free-form body that begins with the topic name (e.g., 'log', 'system', 'firewall', 'dhcp', 'wireless') as the first body word. Distinguishes from rfc3164_unix_proc_pid (which has 'process[pid]:') and asa_bsd_with_pri (which has '%TAG:'). Configurable via 'syslog-time-format=bsd-syslog' (default) or 'iso8601' (which yields '<30>2025-03-16T17:48:26.0000+0200 host body' — same envelope structure with ISO8601 timestamp + 4-digit fractional + tz_offset, no version digit; second canonical example shows this). RouterOS also supports CEF format ('remote-log-format=default' vs 'syslog' — CEF body wrapped in this same envelope). Topic names act as a pseudo-tag in the body but are not part of envelope.
canonical_examples: <30>Mar 16 17:43:09 mt-hap-capsman log action changed by admin
<30>2025-03-16T17:48:26.0000+0200 mt-hap-capsman log action changed by admin

notes: Brocade ICX / FastIron (now CommScope/Ruckus FastIron) format. Distinctive: '<PRI>BSD-ts:SEVERITY_LETTER:MODULE: body'. Severity is encoded as a single letter — D=Debug, I=Info, N=Notice, W=Warning, E=Error, C=Critical, A=Alert, M=Emergency. Module name (DHCPC, OSPF, BGP, STP, etc.) follows. NO hostname in the envelope (most Brocade switches log without explicit hostname field on the wire — local buffer form shown above does not include it). Wire form prepends '<PRI>' but otherwise matches the local buffer form. Doc cites local buffer; '<189>' is reconstructed wire framing.
canonical_examples: <189>Feb 5 01:22:17:D:DHCPC: TFTP unable to download running-configuration

notes: TP-Link Omada SDN Controller format. Envelope is RFC5424-shaped (PRI + version=1) but with NON-COMPLIANT timestamp: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' (space separator instead of 'T', no fractional, no timezone) — RFC5424 mandates ISO8601 with 'T' and 'Z' or offset. TP-Link community thread 'OC200 Syslog message is RFC non-compliant' explicitly flags this. Hostname includes spaces and underscores ('Omada Controller_P1VCP_OC200'). Standard RFC5424 placeholder dashes for appname/procid/msgid ('- - -'). Body is free-form. timestamp_shape='other' because it's neither standard ISO8601 nor BSD.
canonical_examples: <134>1 2025-04-17 01:36:46 Omada Controller_P1VCP_OC200 - - - DHCP Server allocated IP address 192.168.1.109 for the client[MAC: 3e-d3-e4-ab-5d-63]

notes: SONiC (Software for Open Networking in the Cloud — Linux-based switching OS used by Dell, Cisco, Edgecore, Microsoft Azure, etc.) distinctive format: '<PRI>BSD-month-day HH:MM:SS.<microseconds>+TZ_offset YYYY hostname SEVERITY_WORD service#component: :- body'. Six unusual features: (1) microsecond fractional precision ('.528854'); (2) explicit timezone offset on the timestamp; (3) year AFTER timestamp; (4) hostname; (5) severity as a WORD (NOTICE/INFO/WARN/ERROR) not numeric severity in the envelope; (6) service tag uses '#' as separator between SAI/SWSS service and sub-component (e.g., 'syncd#syncd', 'swss#orchagent'); (7) literal ': :-' separator before the body. timestamp_shape='other' due to the BSD-month-with-microsec+TZ combination. Doc shows console form; '<157>' (local6.notice) is reconstructed wire framing typical for SONiC.
canonical_examples: <157>Jul 04 17:22:05.528854+00:00 2023 sonic NOTICE syncd#syncd: :- setPort: setting admin UP on eth1 Eth1/1 portId oid:0x100000001
<157>Jul 04 17:22:05.537401+00:00 2023 sonic NOTICE swss#orchagent: :- doTask: Get port state change notification id:1000000000002 status:1

notes: PAN-OS IETF/RFC5424 mode (alternative to default BSD/RFC3164 panos_bsd_csv). Standard RFC5424 envelope: PRI + version='1' + ISO8601-fractional timestamp + hostname + appname='paloalto' + procid='-' + msgid='-' + structured-data + body. Structured-data block uses non-IANA SDID '[meta version="X.Y.Z"]' carrying PAN-OS firmware version. Body remains pipe-delimited CSV (same as panos_bsd_csv body), version=X.Y.Z|subtype=general|... — body format out of envelope scope. IETF mode typically uses TCP or TLS transport (not UDP) per Palo Alto recommendations. Activated by selecting 'IETF' format in syslog server profile.
canonical_examples: <14>1 2025-10-30T13:13:04.000Z e26secgw02 paloalto - - [meta version="11.2.6"] version=11.2.6|subtype=general|...

notes: FortiGate (Fortinet NGFW) emits TWO distinct envelope variants depending on configuration. (1) PRI-only envelope (FIRST canonical_example): '<PRI>date=YYYY-MM-DD time=HH:MM:SS devname=... devid=... logid=... type=... ...' — no envelope timestamp, no envelope hostname, just PRI then immediately into key=value body. The body fields 'date=' and 'time=' carry the device-local time. timestamp_shape='other' because the envelope itself has no timestamp. This is the form covered by syslog-mega-parser format #13 ('Syslog encoded key value pairs'). (2) Standard RFC3164 envelope (SECOND canonical_example): '<PRI>BSD-ts hostname date=... time=... devname=...' — proper RFC3164 envelope wrapping the KV body. That second form's envelope matches asa_bsd_with_pri shape; body remains the same FortiGate KV pairs. Body format (KV) is out of envelope scope (handled by ParseKeyValue OTTL function elsewhere). The choice between forms is configurable in FortiGate's syslog config — default-of-defaults emits the first form on 'fast-mode' / direct-to-collector configs.
canonical_examples: <189>date=2021-01-15 time=12:58:59 devname="FORTI_111" devid="FG100D3G12801312" logid="0001000014" type="traffic" subtype="local" level="notice"

<134>Sep 5 10:22:33 firewall01 date=2023-09-05 time=10:22:33 devname=FGT-001 devid=FGT-001 logid=0100020000 type=event subtype=system level=notice srcip=192.168.1.50 user="admin" action=login status=success

notes: Sophos UTM 9 (Astaro heritage; distinct from XG/SFOS) uses a NON-STANDARD timestamp format: 'YYYY:MM:DD-HH:MM:SS' — colons separate date components AND time components, dash separates date from time. Wire form: '<PRI>YYYY:MM:DD-HH:MM:SS hostname program[pid]: id="N" KV-body'. Default rsyslog template: '<$PRI>$YEAR:$MONTH:$DAY-$HOUR:$MIN:$SEC $HOST $MSGHDR$MSG'. RFC3164 datetime parsers reject this because the colons-in-date confuse the parser (timestamp_shape='other'). Body uses Sophos-specific KV pairs starting with 'id="NNNN"'. Body subfields vary by event type (firewall, IPS, web filter). Canonical_example reconstructs a typical wire form; no fully-cited single example with PRI was found, but the format template is documented in martinseener's grok pattern and Sophos community threads. UTM 9 reached EOL 2026 but legacy installations remain.
canonical_examples: <134>2022:12:29-11:46:36 utm9host firewall[1234]: id="2001" srcip="10.0.0.1" dstip="8.8.8.8" proto=tcp sport=51234 dport=443 action=allow

notes: pfSense (Netgate) and OPNsense — both FreeBSD-based open-source firewall distributions — emit by DEFAULT in BSD/RFC3164 mode WITHOUT a hostname in the envelope. Wire form: '<PRI>BSD-ts process[pid]: body' — no hostname between timestamp and process tag. Structurally distinct from rfc3164_unix_proc_pid (which has hostname); a parser expecting hostname after timestamp would misparse 'process[pid]:' as the hostname. Documented as a known issue (pfSense bug #7020/#6975 'Hostname is omitted when sending logs on syslog'). pfSense 2.5.0+ adds optional RFC5424 mode that includes hostname; that variant matches ios_xe_rfc5424 envelope shape. The body in this canonical example is the pfSense filterlog CSV format (16777216, em0, match, block, in, 4, ... — IPv4 fields including interface, action, direction, length, protocol, src, dst, ports). Body format out of envelope scope. Same envelope behavior on OPNsense.

F5 BIG-IP TMOS-based modules (LTM, GTM/DNS, APM, ASM/AWAF, AFM) all share this envelope shape. Distinctive: '<PRI> BSD-ts hostname severity_word process[pid]: 8-hex-digit-msg-id :severity-digit: body'. Multiple quirks: (1) SPACE after PRI before timestamp; (2) severity word ('emerg'/'alert'/'crit'/'err'/'warning'/'notice'/'info'/'debug') appears between hostname and process tag — IN ADDITION to the severity already encoded in PRI; (3) 8-character hex message-id from F5's message catalog (e.g., '01070638' for mcpd Pool messages, '23003142' for tmm); (4) ' :digit: ' triple-colon-with-space separator carrying numeric severity (matches the severity_word). Common process names: tmm (Traffic Management Microkernel), mcpd (master config), big3d, gtmd, bigd, monitor processes. Catalog entry covers all BIG-IP modules; sibling F5 products (NGINX Plus, NGINX OSS, F5OS chassis, F5 Distributed Cloud) emit different shapes — see discovered.jsonl entries for those.
canonical_examples: <133> Nov 5 14:01:50 f5networks.bigip.test notice mcpd[5281]: 01070638 :5: Pool /Common/q-qa-pool member /Common/q-qa1:25 session status forced disabled.
<134> Apr 23 08:16:55 f5networks.bigip.test info tmm[1286]: 23003142 :6: Per-invocation log rate exceeded; throttling.

notes: VMware ESXi (and other VMware/vSphere products) emit RFC3164-shaped envelopes but with RFC3339/ISO8601 timestamp instead of standard BSD timestamp — explicitly documented by VMware as 'an exception to the RFC 3164 specification'. Wire form: '<PRI>YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.fffZ hostname process: body'. Distinctive vs ios_xe_rfc5424 because there's NO version digit '1' between PRI and timestamp. Distinctive vs cataloged 3164 entries because timestamp is ISO not BSD. ESXi vmkernel logs add a 'cpuN:NNNNNNN)' world-context prefix at the start of body for kernel events. Process names include vmkernel, hostd, vpxa, fdm, rhttpproxy, vmkwarning, vmksummary. ESXi 8 also supports a separate RFC5424 mode (selectable via 'esxcli system syslog config set --format=rfc5424') which matches ios_xe_rfc5424. This entry covers the default/legacy form. Format also seen as #2 in observIQ syslog-mega-parser.

notes: Avaya Aura Communication Manager (PBX/UC platform) uses a distinctive PERIOD-separated syslog format. Wire form: '<PRI>Mmm.DD.HH:MM:SS.hostname.process:.body' — periods replace standard spaces between fields and within the BSD timestamp itself. Envelope structure is RFC3164-inspired but with the unique period separator that strict RFC3164 parsers will reject. The hostname is short-form local (NOT FQDN per Avaya docs). timestamp_shape='other' due to the period separators that make BSD-shape detection fail. Avaya's own docs confirm this is the actual emitted format. Distinct from any other cataloged shape. Found 2026-05-01 during Phase A high-effort web search.

notes: HPE iLO 4/5/6 emits RFC5424 with PRI and APP-NAME literal '#ILOn' (e.g. '#ILO4', '#ILO5'). MSGID/PROCID/SD are nilvalue '-'. Body is free-text iLO event. SC4S filter program-matches '(#ILO|[iI][lL][oO])\d+' to identify. Timestamp ISO8601 UTC with 'Z'. Distinctive due to '#' prefix on app-name.

notes: NetApp ONTAP audit-log syslog form. Distinctive shape: BSD timestamp + hostname, then a SECOND copy of hostname as the syslog tag (HOST: HOST:), followed by a hex cookie pair, then a fully-printed internal timestamp ('Day Mon dd YYYY HH:MM:SS +TZ'), then '[subsystem<pid>]', then double-colon-delimited audit fields. Body format is double-colon delimited audit context (out of scope). Envelope-only catalog: PRI + BSD ts + doubled host marks the variant.

notes: NetApp ONTAP EMS (Event Management System) syslog form (legacy, pre-new-format). Header is '<PRI>BSD-ts [host:event-category:severity]: ' — colon-delimited triple wrapped in square brackets occupies the syslog-tag slot. Distinctive: no separate hostname token; identity carried inside the bracket. New format also exists as plain RFC5424 (ontap inherits standard 5424 envelope) — that form is intentionally NOT cataloged separately, only this distinctive bracketed-tag form is.

notes: HPE Aruba ArubaOS Mobility Controller legacy syslog form, also emitted by Aruba APs (sapd/stm/authmgr/wms processes). Shape: PRI + BSD timestamp WITH year (Mmm dd HH:MM:SS YYYY) + hostname + program[pid]: + '<error_no> <pid> <SEVERITY_WORD> <host ip>' body-lead. Severity-word values include EMER/ALRT/CRIT/ERR/ERRS/WARN/NOT/INFO/DBUG. Some variants omit the inner pid token and use '<error_no> <SEVERITY_WORD> |context|' shape (sapd example). notes: A10 Networks Thunder/ACOS / vThunder syslog. Envelope is standard rfc3164-style ('<PRI>BSD-ts hostname program: body') with program = a10logd / a10timer / a10stat / a10switch / a10hm / a10rt / a10rip / a10ospf / a10snmpd / a10wa / a10gmpd / a10snpm_trapd / a10lb (per SC4S app-syslog-a10.conf). notes: HPE ProCurve format 2 (classic ProCurve / older firmware). Distinctive: PRI + BSD ts + hostname (often dotted-IP) + 5-digit event id (e.g., '00179') + short tag word + colon + body. Hostname slot may be followed by one or two spaces before the event id. No process[pid] form — the 5-digit-event-id occupies the position where Unix syslog typically has the program name.

notes: HPE ProCurve format 1 (newer firmware, Comware-derived format). Shape parallel to comware_double_pct_module_level_digest entry: PRI + BSD ts (year optional) + hostname + optional '(Stack)' marker + '%%vv<MODULE>/<level>/<DIGEST>(<origin>):' + body. Origin marker '(l)' = log host destination, '(t)' = trap. Distinctive vs Comware: ProCurve fmt1 timestamp uses BSD without colon-fractional seconds (Comware uses ':fff' fractional separator). Year is sometimes present (when 'logging facility ... format' is configured) and sometimes absent.

notes: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise OmniSwitch (AOS) syslog. Envelope: standard rfc3164-like '<PRI>BSD-ts hostname program: body' with program=swlogd. Distinctive AOS body lead: '<subsystem-name> <module> <severity-word>(<level-int>)' (e.g., 'bcmd esm info(5)' = subsystem 'bcmd', module 'esm', severity 'info', level 5). Body is free-form key=value after the subsystem trio. swlogd is the OmniSwitch system log daemon — distinctive vs other rfc3164 emitters.

notes: Riverbed SteelHead WAN-optimization syslog. Envelope: rfc3164 with PRI + BSD ts + hostname + program[pid]: + body. Program = sport (Optimization Service Process Tracking) and similar SteelHead daemons. Distinctive body lead: '[<subsystem>/<sub-component>.<SEVERITY-WORD>] <numeric-id> {<src>:<port> <dst>:<port>} <verb>' — bracket-encoded subsystem path with severity, then numeric id, then brace-grouped endpoint pair. Discovered.jsonl noted RFC5424 from Riverbed Marketplace docs; fixture confirms rfc3164 envelope is also emitted (likely the default; RFC5424 may be configurable).

Ubiquiti UniFi (USW switches, UAP access points) legacy AP-direct syslog (port 514, BSD). DISTINCTIVE: hostname slot is a comma-separated triple '<MODEL>,<MAC-no-colons>,<FIRMWARE-VERSION>' (e.g. 'US8P60,18e8294876c3,v4.0.66.10832' or 'U7PG2,788a2056b181,v4.0.66.10832'). Two sub-shapes: (1) tuple-host followed by ' <program>: <subsystem>: <body>' (no pid bracket) and (2) tuple-host followed by ': <program>[<pid>]: <body>' (Unix-style program[pid]). Confirms the 'distinctive tuple-formatted hostname' previously hypothesized in discovered.jsonl. CEF body is the modern alternative output (when configured for SIEM); body format out of envelope scope.

notes: FRR (FRRouting) embedded in SONiC switching OS — RFC5424 envelope. DISTINCTIVE: APP-NAME slot is a hash-separated double identifier '<frr-daemon>#<frr-subdaemon>' (e.g., 'bgp#zebra' = bgpd process logging through zebra). PROCID is numeric (FRR pid). MSGID and SD are nilvalue '-'. Body often includes FRR's bracketed message-id and EC (Error Class) prefixes '[XXXXX-XXXXX][EC XXXXXXXXX]'. Timestamp ISO8601 with microsecond precision and offset. Cataloged as it appears via Dell SONiC; same shape applies to standalone FRR when configured for RFC5424. Distinct from a generic RFC5424 entry due to '#' in APP-NAME.

notes: HPE Aruba AOS-CX switching OS (replaces AOS-S/ProCurve). Envelope: ISO8601 timestamp with microsecond precision and TZ offset + hostname + Unix-style 'program[pid]:' + body. PRI bracket is OPTIONAL — elastic integrations grok pattern OPTIONAL_PRIORITY matches '<PRI>' optionally followed by RFC5424 version digit. Distinctive AOS-CX body lead: 'Event|<event-id>|<SEVERITY>|<module>|<slot>|<text>' pipe-delimited (similar to ArubaOS controllers' angle-bracket form, but pipe-delimited and no separate severity-word in angle brackets). PROC_ID slot also supports RFC5424-strict ' <pid> - - ' triple instead of '[pid]:'. Distinct from procurve_fmt1 (Comware-derived) and aruba_aos_bsd_year_anglebrackets (ArubaOS legacy controllers).

notes: Huawei VRP (Versatile Routing Platform — switches/routers/firewalls). Shape parallel to comware_double_pct_module_level_digest with VRP-specific quirks: PRI + ' YYYY-M-D HH:MM:SS.<ms>.1 <host> %%<vv><MODULE>/<level>/<DIGEST>(<l|t>)[<msg-id>]:<body>'. Version marker '%%01' (vv=01 for current VRP). Distinctive: dash-separated ymd-date with NO leading zero on month/day, fractional milliseconds with literal '.1' sequence-marker suffix, version marker '%%01', module/level/digest tag, and '(l)' origin (l=loghost, t=trap). notes: DDN Tintri VMstore array (now part of DataDirect Networks). DISTINCTIVE: NO program/tag in rfc-style position — instead a standalone ' : Scrubbed@ ' marker (with surrounding spaces) follows the hostname, then an INNER iso timestamp (microsecond precision with TZ offset), then the actual program name 'tomcat:' (or other). The 'Scrubbed@' literal indicates Tintri's redaction process ran on this log. Body uses thread+class brackets, Java log levels, and audit message-id 'LOG-AUDIT-NNNN'. Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <165>2021-03-22T17:55:01.000Z host : Scrubbed@ 2021-03-22T13:55:01.620956-04:00 tomcat: [https-jsse-nio-443-exec-8,com.tintri.log.LogBase] WARN : USER:AUDIT:LOG-AUDIT-0083: [358966] Reset credentials [POST /api/v310/userAccount/reset#012[Severity:WARNING , Facility:LOCAL6]

notes: Pure Storage FlashArray — JSON-payload form. DISTINCTIVE: 'program' slot in the program[pid]: position is a FULL FILESYSTEM PATH ('/space_utility_json.py') rather than a daemon name. Path is the script invoked by Pure's space-utility scheduler. Body is JSON with 'payload' and 'signature' keys for cryptographic integrity. Distinct from purity:test (separate Pure Storage variant with 'purity.test' as program). Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <182>Oct  3 15:23:39 host /space_utility_json.py[98617]: {"payload": {"effective_used": 111111111111111, "total_extents": 111111111}, "signature": "..."}

notes: Schneider Electric APC NetBotz / Smart-UPS Network Management Card. DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 with PRI + BSD-ts + hostname (often dotted-IP like '10.1.1.1') and NO program/tag — body starts directly with free-text event description, terminating in a hex-encoded event code ('0x0004'). Hostname-as-IP is a common APC default since these embedded NMCs often have no DNS-resolvable name. Trailing hex-code is the APC event identifier. Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <27>Mar 24 21:45:28 10.1.1.1 Detected an unauthorized user attempting to access the SNMP interface from 10.1.1.1 0x0004

notes: Solace PubSub+ message broker. DISTINCTIVE: triple-colon-delimited tag sequence — '<PRI>BSD-ts <host> event: <SUBSYSTEM>: <EVENT_ID>: - - <body>'. Three layers: literal 'event:', then subsystem ('SYSTEM', 'CLIENT', 'VPN', etc.), then specific event-id (e.g. 'SYSTEM_CLIENT_CONNECT_FAIL'). The '- -' double-dash placeholder follows. Body uses 'Message VPN (X) Sol Client...' Solace-specific format. Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <158>Nov 11 15:22:22 xx-09 event: SYSTEM: SYSTEM_CLIENT_CONNECT_FAIL: - - Message VPN (xx) Sol Client username xx clientname xx@RTMD_ALL connect failed from 10.0.0.0:33454 - Forbidden: Client Name Already In Use

notes: Dell EMC PowerStore (modern storage array). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 BSD-ts + hostname + '[<procid>]:' (bracket-only PROCID without program-name preceding), THEN inline-RFC5424-style structured-data — '<inner-iso-ts> <hostname-repeat> <internal-id> <procid>@<hash> <module> [<sd-id@PEN> <kv...>]' followed by free-text body. The inline SD '[PowerStore_audit_event@1139 id="..." user="..." ...]' uses RFC5424 SD-ELEMENT syntax embedded in an rfc3164 envelope. Hostname format includes appliance serial number with B-suffix (failover pair). Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <110>Jan 31 19:43:24 APM00243620939-B [358]: 2025-01-31T19:43:17 APM00243620939-B PSb8ad27c26647 358@HM3CTZ3 Authentication [PowerStore_audit_event@1139 id="2341" user="admin" resource_type="login_session" action="None" client_ip="10.114.173.252" appliance="APM00243620939" status="success"] User "admin" logged in successfully.

notes: Dell RSA SecurID Authentication Manager. DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 PRI + BSD-ts + hostname, then NO program/tag — instead a comma-delimited 4-token preamble: '<inner-ISO-ts-with-comma-ms> <FQDN> <java-logger-path> <LEVEL>' (e.g., 'audit.admin.com.rsa.authmgr.internal.admin.principalmgt.impl.AMPrincipalAdministrationImpl' — fully-qualified Java class) followed by CSV body. Inner timestamp uses comma-fractional-seconds 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS,fff'. Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <14>Mar 25 15:09:33 host 2020-03-25 15:09:33,503, host.example.net, audit.admin.com.rsa.authmgr.internal.admin.principalmgt.impl.AMPrincipalAdministrationImpl, INFO,

<11>Mar 25 15:04:54 host 2020-03-25 15:04:54,485, host.example.net, system.com.rsa.ims.configuration.impl.AuthorizationEnabledConfigurationServiceImpl, ERROR, xxxxx,xxxxx,10.0.0.1,10.0.0.1,CONF_READ,16153,FAIL,INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE,xxxx-fnIz0FpnFNO0,xxxxx,xxx,xxx,xxxx,xxx,xxxx,0000-Global-0000,auth_manager.dashboard.hide.grpagent,,,,,

notes: Dell EMC Avamar deduplication backup. DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 with PRI + BSD-ts + hostname-with-trailing-colon (no separate program slot), body uses XML-style angle-bracket tag markers as field labels: '<Code> 22555 <Type> AUDIT <Severity> PROCESS <Category> SECURITY <User> email@my.com <HwSource> ...'. Tags are open-only (not paired), function as inline field-name markers. Final field is a self-closing XML element '<requestor domain="..." host="..." .../>'. Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <141>Apr 5 22:51:54 amavar: <Code> 22555 <Type> AUDIT <Severity> PROCESS <Category> SECURITY <User> email@my.com <HwSource> amavar <Summary> Changed backup expiration. <path> /clients/Dev-Cert/Windows/test.com <createtime> 2024-02-03 02:32:09 CST <plugin> 3001 <labelnum> 388 <expiration> 2024-02-16 <requestor> <requestor domain="/" host="1.1.1.1" product="MCGUI" role="Administrator" user="email@my.com"/>

notes: SecureAuth Identity Platform (formerly Core Authentication / IdP). RFC5424-strict envelope. DISTINCTIVE: MSGID slot uses 'IDxxxxx' prefix convention (e.g., 'ID90020'), and SD-ELEMENT 'SecureAuth@27389' carries an unusually rich set of vendor-specific keys (UserAgent, RequestID, Realm, Appliance, Company, Version, PEN, HostName, Category, Priority, EventID — 12+ keys). Body is freeform after the SD. PEN 27389 = SecureAuth's IANA-assigned Private Enterprise Number. Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <86>1 2023-06-01T15:57:33.760Z 10.164.2.132 SecureAuth2 2928 ID90020 [SecureAuth@27389 UserAgent="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36" UserHostAddress="10.1.2.2" RequestID="d2014021-4e06-45c6-a580-346e12346b60" Realm="SecureAuth2" Appliance="SecureAuth05VM.domain.com" Company="National Title Group Inc" Version="9.2.0.85" PEN="27389" HostName="10.1.2.2" Category="AUDIT" Priority="4" EventID="90020"] Application - Begin request

notes: Epic EHR (Electronic Health Records) — Interconnect service audit. RFC5424 envelope with PRI + ver=1 + ISO + Z + host + APP-NAME='Epic' + PROCID (numeric) + MSGID='-' + SD='[origin software="..." swVersion="..."]' (uses non-PEN-prefixed SDID 'origin'). Body is XML payload — '<?xml version="1.0"?><EventLog><E1Mid>...IC_SERVICE_AUDIT...</E1Mid><EventCnt>...</EventCnt><EMPid>...^SERVICE,..^...</EMPid>...<Mnemonics>...</Mnemonics></EventLog>'. Caret-delimited compound IDs ('113^SERVICE, INTERCONNECT^ICSVC') are HL7-MLLP convention. Discovery from sweep.
canonical_examples: <85>1 2024-01-19T15:43:19Z host Epic 7652 - [origin software="Security-SIEM" swVersion="10.5.0"] <?xml version="1.0"?><EventLog><E1Mid>IC_SERVICE_AUDIT</E1Mid><EventCnt>1</EventCnt><EMPid>113^SERVICE, INTERCONNECT^ICSVC</EMPid><Source>poc</Source><Action>Query</Action><Date>1/19/2024</Date><Time>10:43:19 AM</Time><Mnemonics><Mnemonic Name="APIID"><Value>1</Value></Mnemonic></Mnemonics></EventLog>

notes: Cisco FWSM and PIX firewalls. DISTINCTIVE vs asa_bsd_with_pri: literal ' : ' (space-colon-space) between hostname and mnemonic. Single envelope variant covers both products.

<111> Apr 5 22:51:54 host : %PIX-3-302022: Built inbound ICMP connection for faddr XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX/1 gaddr XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX/1 laddr XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX/0

notes: Cisco TVCS (TelePresence Video Communication Server, formerly Tandberg VCS). DISTINCTIVE: ISO8601 + Z timestamp in BSD position, then host + space-colon-space + percent-mnemonic.

Cisco UCM (CallManager). Multiple distinctive features: seqnum-prefix after PRI, year-first BSD with AM/PM and dot-fractional ms, standalone TZ word + space-colon-double-space before mnemonic, percent-bracket KV body.

notes: Citrix NetScaler/ADC. Distinctive timestamp: MM/DD/YYYY:HH:MM:SS with literal colon between date and time, GMT word, then host + facility + space-colon-space + protocol/event-type positional + space-colon-space + Context body lead.

notes: pfSense / OPNsense BSD-based firewall. Distinctive: PRI + BSD ts + 'filterlog:' program with NO hostname between. Body is comma-CSV with leading rule-id and ~20 positional fields.

FireEye CMS / NX (Network Security, formerly NX appliance, now Trellix). DISTINCTIVE: degenerate envelope — '<PRI>fenotify-<numeric-id>.<severity-word>: <body>' with NO timestamp and NO hostname. Identity carried in tag 'fenotify-NUM.severity:'. Severity word is one of 'warning'/'alert'/'malware'/etc. Body is typically CEF (out of envelope scope).
canonical_examples: <164>fenotify-1590500.warning: CEF:0|FireEye|CMS|9.0.1.923211|MC|malware-callback|7|requestClientApplication=Mozilla/5.0 cn2Label=sid cn2=11111112 rt=Jan 25 2021 20:37:54 UTC proto=tcp dst=172.65.203.203 externalId=1590500 src=162.18.29.1 dvc=10.246.129.27

notes: Splunk forwarder COOKED format (S2S protocol over syslog). DISTINCTIVE: RFC5424 with TIMESTAMP and HOSTNAME both nilvalue '-' (Splunk preserves real timestamp inside body), APP-NAME='SPLUNK', PROCID='-', MSGID='COOKED', SD='[fields@274489 ...]' (Splunk's PEN 274489) carrying source-host/index/sourcetype/source as KV. Body uses '~~~SM~~~...~~~EM~~~' pre-cooked-fields markers (Splunk Metric/Event start/end markers) followed by the actual log line.
canonical_examples: <1>1 - - SPLUNK - COOKED [fields@274489 t="1627772621.099" h="so1" i="_internal" st="splunkd" s="/opt/splunk/var/log/splunk/metrics.log"] ~~~SM~~~timestartpos::0 timeendpos::29 _subsecond::.099 date_second::41 date_hour::23 date_minute::3 date_year::2021 date_month::july date_mday::31 date_wday::saturday date_zone::0 group::mpool max_used_interval::0 max_used::0 avg_rsv::0 capacity::134217728 used::0 rep_used::0 metric_name::spl.mlog.mpool~~~EM~~~07-31-2021 23:03:41.099 +0000 INFO  Metrics - group=mpool, max_used_interval=0, max_used=0, avg_rsv=0, capacity=134217728, used=0, rep_used=0

notes: Cisco Viptela / SD-WAN (vManage / vBond / vSmart / vEdge). Envelope: rfc3164 PRI + BSD-ts + outer-hostname + program[pid]:. DISTINCTIVE: body lead extends Cisco mnemonic format to embed the LOGGING device's hostname — '%Viptela-<inner-hostname>-<daemon>-<severity>-<msgid>:'. Outer hostname (rfc3164 header) and inner hostname (in mnemonic) often differ, indicating one Viptela device forwarded a log on behalf of another. 'msgid' is a 7-digit numeric event identifier.
canonical_examples: <111> Mar 17 18:35:12 xyz_vManage_West SYSMGR[919]: %Viptela-xyz_vManage_East-sysmgrd-6-INFO-1400002: Notification: 3/17/2022 18:35:12 system-login-change severity-level:minor host-name:"XYZ_vManage_East" system-ip:1.1.1.3 user-name:"mn2c" user-id:2227

notes: Cisco ACS (Access Control Server, AAA/TACACS+/RADIUS server, EOL — replaced by ISE). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 + program 'CSCOacs_<EventClass>:' (Single_Authentications, Multi_Authentications, Failed_Attempts, Passed_Authentications, etc.) followed by 8-token positional preamble: '<10-digit-seq> <total-msg-count> <part-num> <date> <time> <tzoffset> <detail-id> <msg-id-numeric> <LEVEL> <service-class>:' then comma-CSV body. MULTI variants split across multiple syslog messages with continuation form '<seq> <total> <part>' as the only preamble for parts 1+ (no repeated date/level).
canonical_examples: <165> Apr 5 22:51:54 host CSCOacs_Single_Authentications: 0765855540 1 0 2014-06-03 13:05:30 +0530 0178632943 5202 NOTICE Device-Administration: Field1, Field2
<165> Apr 5 22:51:54 host CSCOacs_Multi_Authentications: 0000000002 2 0 2014-06-03 13:05:30 +0530 0000008450 5203 NOTICE Device-Administration: Field1, Field2,
<165> Apr 5 22:51:54 host CSCOacs_Multi_Authentications: 0000000002 2 1 Field3, Field4,

notes: Cisco Meeting Server (formerly Acano CMS — virtual meeting server). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 BSD-ts followed by 'facility.severity' word-pair token (e.g. 'user.info', 'daemon.warning') BEFORE the hostname slot — duplicating the PRI value in human-readable form as part of the message. Then standard hostname + program + body. The redundant facility.severity word-pair before hostname is unique to CMS.
canonical_examples: <11>Jan 16 04:25:44 user.info cms20 authp:  Using authentication server cb_video.cms20.video.uc.lab to authenticate user tyamada@cms20.video.uc.lab (index: 1/1, reason: first match)

notes: Cisco Meeting Management (CMM, web frontend for Cisco Meeting Server). TWO sub-shapes within a single envelope: (1) program[pid]: form — '<PRI>BSD-ts host cmm-<service>_<N>[pid]:  <LEVEL> <inner-iso> <STATUS> | <body> | <module>:<line>' (Java-style log with pipe-delim fields, double-space after pid:colon); (2) audit form — '<PRI>BSD-ts host <inner-date>  <inner-time-comma-ms> - <auth-context> - <body>' (no program/tag, dash-separator structure with auth-context like 'local:user/HTTP/IPvN:ip:proto:port'). Cataloged as single variant covering both.
canonical_examples: <14>Jan 26 14:20:39 host cmm-web_smart_agent_1[273]:  INFO 2022-02-20T18:53:05.123456Z SUCCESS | initializing smart agent v1.2.5 | sapy.manage:19
<14>Jan 26 14:20:39 host 2022-02-20  18:52:22,250 - local:myuser/HTTP/IPv3:1.2.3.4:tcp:50010 - Update configuration successful

notes: NetApp StorageGRID (object storage). Envelope: standard rfc3164 PRI + BSD-ts + hostname + 'Audit:' tag. DISTINCTIVE body (worth cataloging vendor entry despite generic envelope): inner ISO timestamp followed by '[AUDT:' wrapper containing nested-bracket KEY+TYPE+VALUE triples. TYPE codes are FourCC-style ('CSTR' = C-string, 'UI32' = uint32, 'UI64' = uint64, 'FC32' = FourCC code). Keys are 4-character codes like MRMD/MPAT/MDNA/MSIP/MDIP/MUUN/MRSC/RSLT/MRSP/AVER/ATIM/ATYP/ANID/AMID/ATID — distinctive ATIM/AVER/ATYP/ANID prefix pattern.
canonical_examples: <190>Apr 5 22:51:54 host Audit: 2022-11-23T12:02:51.007373 [AUDT:[MRMD(CSTR):"POST"][MPAT(CSTR):"/api/v3/authorize"][MPQP(CSTR):""][MDNA(CSTR):"8.8.8.8"][MSIP(CSTR):"4.4.4.4"][MDIP(CSTR):"4.4.4.4"][MUUN(CSTR):""][MRSC(UI32):200][RSLT(FC32):SUCS][MRSP(CSTR):""][AVER(UI32):10][ATIM(UI64):1669204971007373][ATYP(FC32):MGAU][ANID(UI32):12525832][AMID(FC32):GMGT][ATID(UI64):12690095365319551758]]

product: WSA (Web Security Appliance / Secure Web Appliance)
notes: Cisco WSA (Web Security Appliance, formerly IronPort S-series, now Secure Web Appliance). DISTINCTIVE envelope: rfc3164 PRI + BSD-ts + hostname, then in the program-slot position is a FLOAT EPOCH timestamp ('1539321122.787' = unix-epoch.ms) instead of a program/tag name. The wsatime float is the inner WSA log time, but it occupies the syslog program slot. Body is Squid Common Log Format with bracketed sub-tag '<sub-fields>' and trailing user-agent + size. Multiple SC4S sub-formats (squid 11.7 / l4tm / squid 12.5 / w3c_recommended) all share this envelope shape with body-format variants.

notes: Veeam Backup & Replication via Veeam Management Pack. RFC5424-strict envelope with PRI + ver=1 + ISO8601-microsecond + tz-offset + host + APP-NAME='Veeam_MP' + PROCID='-' + MSGID='-'. DISTINCTIVE: TWO consecutive SD-ELEMENTs — first '[origin enterpriseId="..."]' uses non-PEN-prefixed SDID 'origin' (RFC5424 violation, should be 'origin@PEN'); second bracket '[categoryId=0 instanceId=... OibID=... ... Description="..."]' has NO SDID at all (also RFC5424 violation — pure KV pairs in bracket-position). Both irregularities are characteristic of Veeam MP output.

notes: Netwrix Endpoint Protector (formerly CoSoSys EPP) — DLP/device-control. RFC5424 envelope with DISTINCTIVE APP-NAME slot embedding the source IP — 'EPP-1.1.1.1' (literal 'EPP-' prefix + dotted-quad). Numeric PROCID, MSGID '-', SD '-'. Body is pipe-separated bracket-tag-field markers '[Log ID] value | [Event Name] value | [Client Computer] value | ...'. Body lead summary 'EPP IP - <fqdn> - <category> - <action>:'.

notes: Semperis Directory Services Protector (DSP) — Active Directory threat detection / recovery. DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 + program='Semperis.DSP' (dotted, no [pid]) followed by FIRST bracket as RFC5424-style SD-ELEMENT '[AdChanges@51802]' (Semperis PEN), THEN a sequence of BRACKET-TAGGED FIELD MARKERS '[<FieldName>] <value> [<FieldName>] <value>...' that look like SD but lack SDID structure. Mixed format hybrid.

notes: VMware Carbon Black App Control (formerly Bit9 Parity, now Broadcom). DISTINCTIVE: RFC5424-flavor hybrid — '<PRI>iso-ts host - - - - <body>' with NO version digit between PRI and TIMESTAMP (RFC5424 requires '1' but Carbon Black omits it). Four space-separated nilvalues '-' '-' '-' '-' fill the APP-NAME / PROCID / MSGID / SD slots, followed by 'Carbon Black App Control event:  text="..." type="..." subtype="..."' KV body with double-quoted values.

notes: Thales Vormetric Data Security (DSM, vee-FS, vee-fs). RFC5424-strict envelope. DISTINCTIVE features: (1) APP-NAME slot may have '@<environment>' suffix — 'vee-fs@Prod', 'vee-fs@NonProd', 'dsm@NonProd' — using @ as environment-tag separator (uncommon in RFC5424 APP-NAME). (2) MSGID is alphanumeric vendor code with severity suffix letter — 'CGP2603I' (I=Info), 'CGP2602E' (E=Error), 'COM0313E', 'CGP2610E'. (3) SD-ELEMENT '[CGP@21513 ...]' or '[COM@21513 ...]' uses Vormetric PEN 21513. PROCID is numeric.

notes: Novell Access Manager (NAM, now under OpenText after Micro Focus acquisition; NetIQ branded). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 PRI + BSD-ts + hostname, then NO program/tag — body starts directly with '{' as JSON object. JSON includes 'appName' (literal 'Novell Access Manager'), 'eventId' (vendor numeric code), 'originator', 'component' (e.g., 'nidp'), 'description', 'message' (prefixed with bracketed RFC2822-style date and 'AMDEVICEID#' / 'AMAUTHID#' prefixed identifiers), 'target' (LDAP-DN format).
canonical_examples: <132>Apr 5 22:51:54 host {"appName":"Novell Access Manager","timeStamp":"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z","eventId":"002E0009","subTarget":"c7620505dc4b61cca7665cf1c092ea9980af164691cc5adf88d104dfff18a315","stringValue1":"https://login-test.authbridge-nonprod.example.com/nidp/saml2/metadata","originator":"C423618A1F3FB8F2","component":"nidp","description":"NIDS: Provided an authentication to a remote consumer","message":"[Tue, 15 Jun 2021 02:35:28 +1000]  [Novell Access Manager\\nidp]: AMDEVICEID#C423618A1F3FB8F2: AMAUTHID#c7620505dc4b61cca7665cf1c092ea9980af164691cc5adf88d104dfff18a315: Provided an authentication to a remote consumer on behalf of user...","target":"cn=xxx,ou=users,o=data"}

notes: Sophos Web Appliance / SWA (web filtering / proxy). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 with PRI + BSD-ts + hostname (often dotted-IP) and NO program/tag — body starts directly with KV pairs using SHORT/SINGLE-LETTER keys: 'h=', 'u=', 's=', 'X=', 't=', 'T=', 'Ts=', 'act=', 'cat=', 'in=', 'out=', 'meth=', 'ref=', 'ua=', 'req=', 'dom=', 'rule=', 'reqtime=', 'adtime=', 'dnstime=', etc. Hex categories ('cat="0x220000002a"'), inner unix-epoch ('t=1336666489'), and timing decimals are characteristic.
canonical_examples: <27>Mar 24 21:45:28 10.1.1.1 h=10.99.115.13 u="DOMAIN\johnsmith" s=200 X=- t=1336666489 T=284453 Ts=0 act=1 cat="0x220000002a" app="-" rsn=- threat="-" type="text/html" ctype="text/html" sav-ev=4.77 sav-dv=2012.5.10.4770003 in=1255 out=26198 meth=GET ref="-" ua="Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; rv:12.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/12.0" req="GET http://www.google.ca/ HTTP/1.1" dom="google.ca" filetype="-" rule="0" filesize=25815 axtime=0.048193 fttime=0.049360 scantime=0.011 reqtime=0.027 adtime=0.001625 dnstime=0.000197

notes: Vectra AI Cognito Detect / Recall (NDR — network detection and response). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 PRI + BSD-ts + hostname + program 'vectra_cef' + space + literal '-' + ':' (dash-marker between program and colon). The '-:' construct is unusual — RFC3164 expects 'program:' or 'program[pid]:'. Body is CEF format with Vectra-specific cat='HOST SCORING' and flexNumber labels for threat/certainty/privilege scores.
canonical_examples: <13>Aug 21 09:24:00 S180356X5A19242 vectra_cef -: CEF:0|Vectra Networks|X Series|5.8|hsc|Host Score Change|3|externalId=2765220 cat=HOST SCORING dvc=10.34.252.35 dvchost=host shost=snavpxdevdi2468.corp.firstam.com src=10.32.137.135 dst=10.32.137.135 flexNumber1Label=threat flexNumber1=22 flexNumber2Label=certainty flexNumber2=51 flexNumber3Label=privilege flexNumber3=1 start=1598027040563 end=1598027040563

notes: Akamai Guardicore Centra (segmentation / micro-segmentation, formerly Guardicore standalone). DISTINCTIVE: hybrid envelope — '<PRI><iso-minute-precision>Z <hostname> CEF:0|...' with NO version digit and NO seconds in timestamp ('YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MMZ' — minute precision only, missing seconds component which is unusual for ISO8601 syslog). Hostname slot is literal 'Guardicore'. No program/tag — body is CEF directly.
canonical_examples: <14>2025-12-22T17:17Z Guardicore CEF:0|Guardicore|Centra|51|Network Log|Network Log|None|

notes: Forcepoint Websense Web Security / Triton (web filtering / proxy). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 with PRI + BSD-ts + hostname and NO program/tag — body starts directly with 'vendor=Websense' KV pair followed by a hex marker '9f' standalone token, then 'product=...' and a long sequence of unquoted KV pairs (action, severity, category, user, src_host, dst_host, http_method, etc.). The unquoted hex marker between 'vendor=Websense' and 'product=' is unusual.
canonical_examples: <134>Oct 16 12:13:06 sourcehost2 vendor=Websense 9f product=Security product_version=7.7.0 action=permitted severity=7 category=755 user=LDAP://user7 OU=Users,OU=Beijing,DC=com/TEST\, TEST_NAME src_host=10.0.0.4 src_port=61435 dst_host=HOST-013 dst_ip=10.0.0.19 dst_port=25404 bytes_out=4074 bytes_in=12328 http_response=200 http_method=POST http_user_agent=Mozilla/3.0 url=http://test_web.com/contents/content1.jpg unknownfield=-

notes: Broadcom Symantec DLP (Data Loss Prevention, formerly Symantec Vontu). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 with PRI + BSD-ts + hostname (often appliance name) and NO program/tag — body uses Title_Case_Underscored keys (Application_Name, Attach_File_Name, Endpoint_Location, Incident_id, Policy_Rules, etc.) with values quoted using SMART/CURLY UNICODE QUOTES (U+201D right-double-quotation-mark) rather than ASCII double-quotes. Smart-quote usage breaks standard CEF/JSON parsers — Symantec DLP is one of the few syslog producers that emits curly-quoted values.
canonical_examples: <8>Dec  2 01:11:53  C3068275967 Application_Name=”N/A” Application_User=”N/A” Attach_File_Name=”[UNKNOWN VARIABLE: ATTACHMENT_FILE_NAME]” Blocked=”Passed” Data_Owner=”N/A”  DataOwner_Email=”N/A” Destination_IP=”20.189.173.9 ” Endpoint_Location=”Off the Corporate Network” Incident_id=”6937” Machine_IP=”10.160.165.240” Match_Count=”1” Occured_On=”December 2, 2021 1:11:41 AM” Policy_Name=”test 2” Policy_Rules=”rule 1” Protocol=”TCP:SSL” Severity=”1:High” Status=”New”

notes: Broadcom Symantec ProxySG / Bluecoat (web proxy, formerly Bluecoat ProxySG). Envelope: '<PRI><iso-fractional>Z <hostname> bluecoat[0]: SPLV5.1 <KV body>' — DISTINCTIVE features: (1) standard RFC5424-like timestamp but no version digit; (2) program 'bluecoat[0]:' with hardcoded pid=0; (3) literal 'SPLV5.1' format-version marker after program (Bluecoat's Splunk-targeted log format version); (4) body uses HYPHENATED KV keys following Bluecoat's web-log convention — 'c-ip', 'cs-bytes', 'cs-host', 'cs-method', 'rs-status', 's-action', 'sc-status', 'time-taken', 'x-bluecoat-*' prefix family.
canonical_examples: <134> 2019-08-21T17:42:08.000Z host bluecoat[0]: SPLV5.1 c-ip=192.0.0.6 cs-bytes=6269 cs-categories="unavailable" cs-host=gg.hhh.iii.com cs-ip=192.0.0.6 cs-method=GET cs-uri-path=/Sample/abc-xyz-01.pqr_sample.crt cs-uri-port=8000 cs-uri-scheme=http cs-User-Agent="ocspd/1.0.3" cs-username=user4 rs-status=0 s-action=TCP_HIT s-ip=10.0.0.6 sc-bytes=9469 sc-filter-result=OBSERVED sc-status=200 time-taken=20 x-bluecoat-appliance-name="10.0.0.6-sample_logs" x-bluecoat-transaction-uuid=35d24c931c0erecta-0003000012161a77e70-00042100041002145cc859ed c-url="http://randomserver:8000/en-US/app/examples/"

notes: Arista NGFW (acquired Untangle, runs Untangle UVM platform). DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 PRI + BSD-ts + literal SEVERITY WORD ('INFO', 'WARN', 'ERROR') replacing the hostname slot, followed by 'uvm[0]:' program with hardcoded pid=0 (UVM is the Untangle Virtual Machine daemon — Java app). Body is JSON with vendor-specific keys (timeStamp inner-iso-with-ms, flagged, blocked, sessionId, ruleId, class — fully-qualified Java class name).
canonical_examples: <174>May 22 16:32:28 INFO  uvm[0]:  {"timeStamp":"2023-05-22 16:32:28.771","flagged":false,"blocked":false,"sessionId":110221865377229,"ruleId":0,"class":"class com.untangle.app.firewall.FirewallEvent"}

notes: Cisco Nexus (NX-OS) data center switching. DISTINCTIVE: '<PRI>: <year> <Mon> <dd> <HH:MM:SS> <TZ-word>: %<MODULE>-<SEV>-<MNEMONIC>:' — literal ':' immediately after PRI (no space), no hostname, year-first BSD timestamp, TZ-word standalone token before percent-mnemonic. Similar to cisco_ucsm_colon_after_pri_year_first envelope but UCSM also has nested bracket-key body whereas Nexus has plain mnemonic body. The 'last message repeated N times' form is also seen — uses the same envelope.
canonical_examples: <189>: 2023 May  2 12:57:15 UTC: %ETHPORT-5-IF_DOWN_ADMIN_DOWN: Interface Ethernet1/42 is down (Administratively down)
<185>: 2023 May  2 12:57:15 UTC: %EARL-SW2_DFC1-1-EXCESSIVE_PARITY_ERROR: EARL 0: Parity error detected in VRAM
<187>: 2023 May  2 12:55:19 UTC: last message repeated 1 times

notes: Cisco Aironet WLC (legacy Wireless LAN Controller running IOS-XE wireless). DISTINCTIVE: '<PRI><hostname>: *<TaskName>: <BSD-ts>.<ms>: %<MODULE>-<SEV>-<MNEMONIC>: [PA]<source-file>:<line> <body>' — multiple distinctive features: (1) hostname immediately after PRI without leading space, terminated by ':' (no separator); (2) asterisk-prefixed task name token '*Dot1x_NW_MsgTask_4', '*dtlArpTask' (Cisco wireless thread/task identifier); (3) BSD timestamp with millisecond precision and trailing colon; (4) Cisco mnemonic with '[PA]' literal marker (Process Audit?) before source-file:line body lead. Traceback variant uses '-Traceback:' instead of mnemonic. Mega-parser 'AP Logs 2' real example (hostname: *task: BSD.frac: %LOG-4-Q_IND) appended 2026-06-12.
canonical_examples: <132>WLC001: *Dot1x_NW_MsgTask_4: Sep 06 23:08:09.371: %LOG-4-Q_IND: [PA]dtl_net.c:3393 STA(Target MAC Address) [2c:6d:c1:f5:0c:80, 192.168.2.2] ARP (op ARP REQUEST) received with invalid SPA(Source IP Address) 169.254.161.111
<132>WLC001: *dtlArpTask: Sep 06 22:42:10.514: %DTL-4-ARP_ORPHANPKT_DETECTED: [PA]dtl_net.c:3174 STA(Target MAC Address) [66:7c:de:ef:d9:18, 0.0.0.0] ARP (op ARP REQUEST) received
<132>7777-ABX1340-01: *spamApTask3: May 20 15:09:15.005: %LOG-4-Q_IND: [SA]capwap_ac_sm.c:2053 Ignoring discovery request received on a wrong VLAN (6) on interface (13) from AP 8D-C6-86-9D-08-3C

notes: Cisco Secure Email Gateway / ESA (formerly IronPort) Advanced Malware Protection (AMP) feature logs. DISTINCTIVE: rfc3164 with PRI + BSD-ts + program 'amp:' (or other ESA daemon: mail_logs, ftp, http) and NO HOSTNAME slot — body starts with capitalized LEVEL word ('Info:', 'Notice:', 'Warning:', 'Critical:') followed by free text or KV body. Different from earlier ESA variants which include hostname; this AMP form omits it. Earlier ESA fixture ('<bsd> <host> <app>: Thu Aug 07 ... 2020 Info: ...') has both host and inner-timestamp body lead — this AMP variant has neither.
canonical_examples: <166>Mar 17 18:24:37 amp: Info: File reputation query initiating. File Name = 'mod-6.exe', MID = 5, File Size = 1673216 bytes, File Type = application/x-dosexec

<166>Mar 17 18:24:37 amp: Info: Response received for file reputation query from Cloud. FileName = 'mod-6.exe', MID = 5, Disposition = MALICIOUS, Malware = W32.061DEF69B5-100.SBX.TG, sha256 =061def69b5c100e9979610fa5675bd19258b19a7ff538b5c2d230b467c312f19

<166>Mar 17 18:24:37 amp: Info: File Analysis complete. SHA256: 16454aff5082c2e9df43f3e3b9cdba3c6ae1766416e548c30a971786db570bfc, Submit Timestamp: 1475825466, Disposition: 3 Score: 100, run_id: 194926004

notes: Fortinet FortiMail. DISTINCTIVE: '<PRI><body>' with NO timestamp / hostname / program in syslog header — body starts immediately after PRI with KV pairs separated by COMMAS (not spaces as in FortiGate's KV body). First two KV pairs carry the timestamp ('date=YYYY-MM-DD,time=HH:MM:SS.mmm'). 'pri=' KV inside body is a literal text field (NOT the syslog PRI which is in the bracket prefix). Distinct from fortigate_pri_only_kv_envelope which uses space-separated KV.
canonical_examples: <190>date=2023-01-30,time=16:09:16.825,device_id=FEVM02TM23000064,log_id=0300003065,type=spam,subtype=default,pri=information,session_id="q6OL7fsQ018870-q6OL7fsR018870",client_name="",client_ip="192.168.100.1",dst_ip="10.50.2.225",from="user1@example.com",to="user2@example.com",subject="Test1516",msg="mailfilterd: Starting"

product: PAS / Privileged Access Security (Vault, elastic format)
notes: CyberArk Privileged Access Security (PAS) Vault — 'elastic' configurable output format. DISTINCTIVE: RFC5424-degenerate envelope — '<PRI>1 <iso>Z <hostname> {JSON body}' with NO APP-NAME, NO PROCID, NO MSGID, NO SD elements between hostname and body. Goes directly from hostname to JSON. JSON 'syslog.audit_record' wraps a flat KV payload with Title-Case keys and includes 'Rfc5424' field declaring conformance — but the actual envelope omits the four required RFC5424 nilvalue slots. Distinct from cyberark CEF-body form (firing 76) which is RFC5424-strict + CEF body.
canonical_examples: <5>1 2021-03-08T18:24:49Z VAULT {"format":"elastic","version":"1.0","syslog":{"audit_record":{"Rfc5424":"yes","Timestamp":"Mar 08 10:24:49","IsoTimestamp":"2021-03-08T18:24:49Z","Hostname":"VAULT","Vendor":"Cyber-Ark","Product":"Vault","Version":"11.7.0000","MessageID":"105","Desc":"Add File Category","Severity":"Info","Issuer":"Administrator","Action":"Add File Category","Safe":"Test","File":"Root\\Operating System-WinDesktopLocal-Address-adriansr","Station":"127.0.0.1","Category":"Address","Reason":"Value=[Address]","Message":"Add File Category","GatewayStation":"10.0.1.20"}}}

notes: Cisco ASA RFC3164-like form with a year embedded in the BSD timestamp ('Mmm dd YYYY HH:MM:SS:') and NO hostname field — the %ASA tag follows the colon directly. PRI is optional. Distinct from the no-year ASA variants in this catalog. User-hand-curated real-world format from the observIQ syslog mega-parser (seen in the wild).

Cisco-style access-point/WLC log with a leading pair of message/sequence counters ('NNNNNN: NNNNNN:'), a fractional BSD timestamp with a trailing timezone token (e.g. SST), no hostname, and a %SEC mnemonic tag. Redaction placeholders (xx) left in the body; the envelope is intact. User-hand-curated real-world format from the observIQ syslog mega-parser (seen in the wild).
canonical_examples: <190>363041: 363044: Nov 7 14:38:47.482 SST: %SEC-xx-xxxxxxx: list SNMP denied xx.xx.xx.xx xxx packets

notes: Canonical RFC3164 BSD-syslog envelope (PRI, BSD timestamp, hostname, tag, message). The mega-parser carries this as its baseline rfc3164 case. User-hand-curated real-world format from the observIQ syslog mega-parser (seen in the wild).
canonical_examples: <34>Oct 11 22:14:15 mymachine su: 'su root' failed for lonvick on /dev/pts/8

notes: Canonical RFC5424 IETF-syslog envelope (PRI, version, ISO8601 timestamp, hostname, appname, procid '-', msgid, structured-data '-', BOM-prefixed UTF-8 message). The mega-parser carries this as its baseline rfc5424 case. User-hand-curated real-world format from the observIQ syslog mega-parser (seen in the wild).
canonical_examples: <85>1 2023-04-05T16:20:56Z loki.example.com su - ID47 - BOM'su root' failed for lonvick on /dev/pts/8

notes: RFC5424 message preceded by an RFC6587 octet-counting frame length ('757 '), nil-value header fields ('- - - -'), and a PAN-OS/Ruckus comma-separated TRAFFIC CSV body. Octet framing is a stream-transport concern; a per-message parser sees the leading count as a token. User-hand-curated real-world format from the observIQ syslog mega-parser (seen in the wild).

Meraki-style log with a full RFC5424-ish header (PRI, version '1') in front of a fractional UNIX-epoch timestamp, optionally preceded by an RFC6587 framing octet. Distinct from the bare-epoch Meraki variant in this catalog (no PRI, no version). Redaction placeholders (xx) left in the body; envelope is intact. User-hand-curated real-world format from the observIQ syslog mega-parser (seen in the wild).
